questions.text: Which cost remains constant in total regardless of production volume within the relevant range?
questions.explanation: Fixed costs stay constant in total within the relevant range, while variable costs change proportionally with activity.

questions.text: What does a favorable direct materials price variance indicate?

options.text: Materials were purchased at a lower price than standard
More materials were used than expected

questions.explanation: A favorable price variance means the actual purchase price was less than the standard price.

questions.text: In activity-based costing, costs are allocated using:
questions.explanation: ABC uses multiple activity-based cost drivers to allocate overhead more accurately than a single volume-based driver.

options.text: Sales price minus variable cost per unit
Fixed cost divided by units sold

questions.explanation: Contribution margin per unit is selling price per unit minus variable cost per unit.
The master budget integrates operating budgets (sales, production, etc.) and financial budgets (cash, capital).
